danger_signals.visual_form: Red-colored enemy ants in colony view and underground tunnels
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat faction; direct combat enemy to player's black ant colony

danger_signals.visual_form: Large descending objects or tremors in yard view
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; uncontrollable destructive force threatening colony

danger_signals.visual_form: Toxic cloud or spray visual effect in affected areas
danger_signals.meaning: Chemical hazard; lethal environmental threat to all ants

danger_signals.visual_form: Large mechanical equipment crossing yard surface
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic environmental destruction; eliminates surface terrain

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger predatory creatures rendered in yard/tunnel views
danger_signals.meaning: Predator threat; requires group tactics or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Hazard visual marker in underground or surface areas
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental electrocution risk; lethal contact hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Water effects cascading through tunnels; pheromone trails disappearing
danger_signals.meaning: Communication and navigation disruption; potential drowning hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled ant separated from colony groups
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; isolated ant at increased combat risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple black ants grouped together in same location
safety_signals.meaning: Strength in numbers; colony coordination and mutual defense

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual marking/trail connecting ant locations to resources
safety_signals.meaning: Active communication network; safe navigation pathway established

safety_signals.visual_form: Visible food particles or pheromone trails leading to food
Complex underground chamber system with multiple passages
safety_signals.meaning: Colony establishment; distributed safe havens and growth

safety_signals.visual_form: Queen ant visible in central breeding chamber
safety_signals.meaning: Reproductive capacity; colony continuity and expansion potential

safety_signals.visual_form: Side-view depiction of secure underground rooms away from surface
safety_signals.meaning: Protected from surface hazards; sheltered breeding and storage areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red ant colony serves as primary antagonist faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Black ant pheromone trails; underground chambers with black ant presence; surface territory marked by black ant activity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red-colored ants; distinct visual rendering from black ants
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinguished from player ants; appears in SNES scenarios in 'various locales'
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Red ant pheromone trails in competing territories; rival underground colonies; territorial control indicators

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not directly controllable; represented through environmental hazards (footsteps, bug spray, lawnmower)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: House interior and exterior; yard boundaries; hazard sources (electrical outlets, pesticide application zones)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct creature types; larger scale than individual ants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Predator dens or locations; patrol paths visible through creature behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Small patch of yard with minimal black ant presence; sparse underground tunnel network; top-down perspective with clear surface terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ants foraging for initial food sources; establishing first pheromone trails; limited defensive capability

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active territorial conflict (black vs red ants)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overlapping pheromone trails of both colors; ants clustered in border areas; combat activity animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Worker ants following opposing pheromone trails; combat encounters at territorial boundaries; groups organizing against enemy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead map grid expanding with new colony squares; multiple distinct ant nests visible; young queens and drones visible in breeding chambers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Production of reproductive castes; colonization of new yard sectors; territorial claims on unexplored map areas

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Threat imminent (human activity or predator presence)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large hazard objects descending on surface; predator creature models visible; spray or electrical hazard visual markers appear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ants flee toward underground; defensive clustering; disrupted pheromone following patterns; chaos in ant movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Water cascading through tunnels; visual fading/dissolution of pheromone trail markers; flooding in low-lying chambers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ants lose navigation orientation; increased random wandering; communication network temporarily severed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Absence of red ants from contested territories; black ant pheromone trails dominant across map; visual clearing of enemy colony markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unrestricted expansion; ant foraging extends into previously contested areas; reduced defensive clustering; focus shifts to resource gathering

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Human house infiltration (Full Game objective phase)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map expands to include house structure; interior spaces become accessible; new environmental hazards appear indoors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Colony spreads into house sections; foraging for new food sources indoors; defensive operations against human removal attempts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Drastically reduced ant population visible; empty or sparsely populated chambers; minimal pheromone trail activity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining ants in survival mode; reduced organizational efficiency; queen potentially isolated or eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: Yard view with surface view underneath showing simultaneous surface threats (human footsteps, lawnmower) and underground colony response (ants retreating into chambers)
Visual trails connecting ant locations to food sources, enemy colonies, or hazards; show implicit story of where colony has explored and what threats/resources exist
Spider dens, antlion pits, electrical outlets, and pesticide zones positioned throughout yard tell implicit story of suburban dangers requiring strategic navigation
Presence/absence of queens, drones, and workers in chambers shows colony lifecycle progression and reproductive success story
Map layout with red ant colony in opposing territory; visual proximity and overlap of pheromone trails show implicit territorial dispute story
House structure, yard boundaries, and hazard objects rendered at massive scale relative to ants; communicates human world dominance and environmental pressure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to player input; leaves pheromone trails when directed; other ants follow its pheromone markers
Susceptible to predator attacks; cannot command group support; pheromone trails weaker or ignored by distant ants
Move in coordinated patterns along yellow ant's trails; cluster toward food sources; exhibit organized group behavior
Cluster together when predators or red ants present; converge on threat location; attempt group attack strategies
Red ants follow their own pheromone trails expanding territory; engage in combat with black ants at borders; claim new ground
Red ants concentrate in remaining territory; reduced territorial reach; pheromone trails contract; visible population decline
Stationary or slow movement in predefined territory; becomes visually distinct threat; requires group ant response or avoidance
Rapid movement toward ant clusters; kills individual or small groups; triggers immediate colony alarm behavior
Stationary in central chamber; surrounded by workers; produces drones and new queens; visible indicator of colony health
Potential isolation if colony walls breach; loss of queen triggers loss condition; protection becomes visible priority for workers
Random wandering; pheromone trails washed away; coordination breaks down temporarily; visible colony confusion

notes: SimAnt uses color differentiation (black vs red) as primary faction identification. The dual perspective (top-down yard view with side-view underground chambers) is central to communicating spatial world-state. Pheromone trails serve as both mechanical system and visual narrative device—they show colony organization, threat awareness, and resource discovery. The game communicates danger primarily through environmental hazards (human-caused and natural) rather than dramatic visual effects, reflecting the game's design philosophy of being 'non-intimidating' and 'lighthearted.' Yellow ant highlighting provides clear player agency signal. The progression from Quick Game (single patch conflict) to Full Game (map-based expansion toward house infiltration) uses spatial expansion as visual victory narrative. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on implicit hazard placement and hazard avoidance patterns rather than explicit narrative elements.
